NorthBridge Bank — Module 1, Lesson 3
The Four-Step Investigation Framework
Select a numbered circle to jump to any step
1 OBSERVE
2 HYPOTHESISE
3 TEST
4 RESULT
The Investigation Map
Every engineer follows the same four steps to move from a customer symptom all the way to the root cause. Select a numbered circle above — or tap Start to begin from Step 1.
Step 1 of 4
Observe the Symptom
Confirm exactly what is happening — using only what you can verify.
Do not guess. Do not interpret. Only observe and write down confirmed facts.
🏠 NorthBridge — Monday Morning
The team makes three confirmed observations:

  ✓  Homepage loads — the Internet path is working
  ✓  Login page loads — the Web Server is responding
  ✗  Login request fails — something deeper in the chain has broken

Just by observing, the team has already eliminated four components — without touching a single server.
Step 2 of 4
Form a Hypothesis
Name one specific, testable cause. A hypothesis is not a guess —
it is a statement: "If X is failing, then we will see Y."
🏠 NorthBridge — Two Suspects Remain
Only two components are still in question:

  Path A — App Server failing?
  If true — App Server logs will show high CPU or error responses.

  Path B — Database unreachable?
  If true — App Server logs will show "Database connection timeout."

The team tests Path A first — it is the simplest to confirm or eliminate.
Step 3 of 4
Test the Hypothesis
Run the simplest possible test that gives a clear answer.
Not the most complete test — the shortest path to a clear result.
🏠 NorthBridge — Pulling the Logs
The team pulls the Application Server monitoring logs from 09:00–09:30 AM.

They check three things only:
  ▪  CPU usage during the incident window
  ▪  Error type on every failed login request
  ▪  Whether the App Server was responding at all

No server access required. Monitoring data only. Test completed in under two minutes.
Step 4 of 4
Read the Result & Decide
Confirmed? Document and fix.  Eliminated? Move to the next hypothesis.
Every result — even a negative one — moves the investigation forward.
🏠 NorthBridge — Root Cause Found
Log results from the Application Server:

  ✓  CPU: normal — App Server is healthy
  ✓  App Server responding — Path A eliminated
  ✗  Every login attempt: "Database connection timeout"

Path B confirmed. The Database was unreachable.
Proximate cause identified. But the investigation is not yet closed.
⚠️ ALERT — Open Question — Investigation Continues
A configuration change was pushed to the Database server at 09:00 AM Monday — 37 minutes before the first failures. Made from an internal service account. No one on the database team authorised this change.
Step 1 of 4
Key Principle
